intro: Bulbostylis quaternella, also known as quaternella sedge or false club-rush, is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bulbostylis quaternella include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into remedies such as syrups and tinctures.
